title: Gaza War Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: The Gaza ceasefire entered its 901st day of the war tenuously intact, as Israeli airstrikes killed at least nine Palestinians during Eid al-Fitr and 687 total since the October 2025 ceasefire. The Board of Peace formally presented Hamas with a phased disarmament proposal on March 21, with envoy Nickolay Mladenov briefing the UN Security Council on March 24 and warning that failure could leave Gaza permanently divided. The Rafah crossing reopened on March 19 after a 20-day closure tied to the Israel-Iran war, allowing limited medical evacuations, though Kerem Shalom remains the sole cargo crossing and aid access is critically constrained. OHCHR warned on March 19 that Israel's aid restrictions are inflicting severe gender-specific harm on women and girls. Cumulative Palestinian deaths in Gaza now stand at 72,253 confirmed killed, with 171,912 injured since October 7, 2023.

title: Gaza War Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: The US-brokered ceasefire continues to hold as Gaza enters day 877 of conflict. The ICJ has ordered Israel to ensure basic needs are met for Gaza's population and to support UNRWA operations. All 251 original hostages are now accounted for following the recovery of the last hostage Ran Gvili in January 2026. Israel suspended 37 humanitarian organizations in December 2025, but reopened the Kerem Shalom crossing for fuel and aid on March 3. ICC arrest warrants remain outstanding for Netanyahu and Gallant on charges of war crimes.
